title: Overloading
summary: A piece doing two jobs will fail at one

steps.title: Too Much Work
steps.text: An overloaded piece is defending (or attacking) more than it can handle. The classic case: one piece defends two squares. When you attack one, the other falls. Here Black's queen is defending the king — she can't move without losing to Rxe7+.

steps.hint: The queen's double duty (defending king AND her square) means she dies.

steps.title: Spotting Overloads
steps.text: Look for pieces defending multiple things — pawns defending each other plus a piece, or a knight holding back a file while also blockading a passed pawn. When you find one, attack one of its duties.
